prompt: Just did `git pull` on my spyglass checkout at `~/spyglass/` — new commit landed, I can see a class `NewlyAddedThing` in `src/spyglass/spikesorting/v1/` that wasn't there before. But when I `import spyglass` in Jupyter, I hit `AttributeError: module 'spyglass.spikesorting.v1' has no attribute 'NewlyAddedThing'`. Tracebacks point at `/opt/miniforge/envs/spyglass/lib/python3.11/site-packages/spyglass/spikesorting/v1/...`. What's going on?
behavioral_checks: Identifies site-packages shadowing as the root cause (not a spyglass code bug) — does NOT recommend filing a bug or waiting for the next release; the fix is user-side (`pip install -e .` from the checkout)
Includes the `pip install -e .` step AND the Jupyter kernel restart
Mentions uninstalling `spyglass-neuro` if both installs coexist
